title: RCC: full migration from Node.js to Rust — no JS services remaining
description: jkh directive: no Node.js services after this migration. Everything becomes Rust (server) or WASM (client), with a clean client/server boundary. The Rust foundation already exists — dashboard-server (Axum), squirrelchat-server (Axum/SQLite), dashboard-ui (Leptos/WASM). The migration is: retire rcc/api/index.mjs, replace with a Rust API server that has feature parity, then delete all Node.js service files.

rust_already_exists: rcc/dashboard/dashboard-server/ — Axum server, proxies RCC API + serves WASM
rcc/dashboard/squirrelchat-server/ — Axum + SQLite, WebSocket, full chat server
node_services_to_retire: squirrelchat/server.mjs — superseded by squirrelchat-server Rust crate
rcc/brain/index.mjs (299 lines) — work pump, folds into Rust API
rcc/scout/github.mjs (308 lines) — GitHub integration, folds into Rust API
rcc/vector/index.mjs — Milvus dedup, becomes Rust client
squirrelbus/receive-server.mjs — bus receiver, folds into Rust API
rcc-hub/ — entire directory is a mirror of rcc/api/ — DELETE
node_scripts_to_evaluate: workqueue/scripts/*.mjs — ~30 scripts, evaluate each: keep as one-shot CLI scripts (OK), retire if functionality moves into Rust API, or rewrite as Rust CLI tools
rcc/exec/agent-listener.mjs — remote exec daemon, evaluate: rewrite in Rust or keep as thin shim

rcc-server (new Rust/Axum binary).storage: SQLite (same pattern as squirrelchat-server — already proven)
rcc-server (new Rust/Axum binary).brain: async background task in same process (tokio spawn), not a separate service
rcc-server (new Rust/Axum binary).scout: async background task (GitHub polling on interval)
rcc-server (new Rust/Axum binary).bus: SQLite-backed message store with SSE/WS broadcast

dashboard-ui (existing Leptos/WASM).change: minor — update API base URLs if needed, otherwise unchanged
dashboard-ui (existing Leptos/WASM).note: Already talks to dashboard-server which proxies to rcc-server

squirrelchat-server (existing Rust).change: retire squirrelchat/server.mjs (Node), squirrelchat-server Rust is already production
squirrelchat-server (existing Rust).note: Auth system (wq-SC-auth-001) should target the Rust server, not the Node one

dashboard-server (existing Rust).change: proxy target changes from Node rcc/api to new rcc-server binary
dashboard-server (existing Rust).note: Minimal change — update RCC_URL env var

migration_phases.name: Foundation — rcc-server skeleton
work: Create rcc/rcc-server/ Rust crate (Axum + SQLite + tokio)
Implement /health endpoint
Implement SQLite schema: agents, heartbeats, queue, projects, secrets, bus_messages
Implement /api/agents/* and /api/heartbeat/* (simplest, most critical for fleet health)
Wire into dashboard-server as RCC_URL target
Run alongside existing Node API (shadow mode — both running, compare outputs)

migration_phases.name: Queue + Brain
work: Implement /api/queue/* with full CRUD + claim/complete/fail lifecycle
Implement brain as tokio background task: poll queue, route by capabilities, claim + dispatch
Implement /api/secrets/* (simple key/value store in SQLite)
Feature parity test against existing Node API

migration_phases.name: SquirrelBus + Scout
work: Implement /bus/* with SQLite backing + SSE broadcast
Implement GitHub scout as background task (replaces rcc/scout/github.mjs)
Implement /api/projects/*
Implement /api/exec/* (remote exec dispatch)

migration_phases.name: Auth + User management
work: Port wq-SC-auth-001 auth system to rcc-server (Rust, lettre for email)
Implement /api/auth/* in Rust
Wire Leptos frontend auth gate

migration_phases.name: Cutover + cleanup
work: Switch dashboard-server RCC_URL to rcc-server
Run Node API in read-only mode for 24h (smoke test)
Retire rcc/api/index.mjs
Retire squirrelchat/server.mjs
Delete rcc-hub/ entirely
Delete rcc/brain/, rcc/scout/, rcc/vector/ Node.js modules
Delete rcc/crush-server/, rcc/wasm-dashboard/, rcc/heartbeat-local/
Evaluate workqueue/scripts/*.mjs one by one

tokenhub_audit.note: jkh directive: same audit applies to tokenhub. File as separate task wq-TOKENHUB-audit-001, fire in 1 hour from now (2026-04-01T06:45:00Z). tokenhub has no significant active clients other than RCC fleet — loom never reached production. Potentially the same Rust migration applies.
tokenhub_audit.tokenhub_location: do-host1:8090, source likely in ~/Src or separate repo — Rocky to confirm

risks: rcc/api/index.mjs has undocumented behavior accumulated over multiple generations — full feature parity requires careful audit (wq-RCC-audit-001 started this)
workqueue/scripts/*.mjs are openclaw-invoked cron scripts — rewriting them in Rust may not be worth it if openclaw can call a REST endpoint instead
Brain logic is complex (capability routing, stale claim expiry, dedup) — must not regress
SQLite migration: existing data in JSON files needs a one-time import at cutover
open_questions: Should rcc-server be a new crate or extend dashboard-server? (dashboard-server already exists and proxies everything — could merge them)
workqueue/scripts/*.mjs: rewrite in Rust, keep as Node CLI scripts, or replace with API calls?
What is the tokenhub source location? Rocky to confirm.
